hints: Use type(value) to get the type of a value, then compare it: if type(value) == str: return "string"
You'll need four separate if statements — one for each type. Each one checks the type and returns the matching label.
The tricky part: bool is a subtype of int in Python, which means type(True) == int is actually True. To avoid this, put your bool check first — before the int check.
If identify_type(True) is returning 'integer', your bool check is either missing or coming after the int check. Move if type(value) == bool to be the very first check in the function.
